speaker: WARWICK
text: Clifford of Cumberland, ’tis Warwick calls!

speaker: WARWICK
text: An if thou dost not hide thee from the bear,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Now, when the angry trumpet sounds alarum

speaker: WARWICK
text: And dead men’s cries do fill the empty air,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Clifford, I say, come forth and fight with me;

speaker: WARWICK
text: Proud northern lord, Clifford of Cumberland,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Warwick is hoarse with calling thee to arms.

speaker: WARWICK
text: How now, my noble lord? What, all afoot?

speaker: YORK
text: The deadly-handed Clifford slew my steed,

speaker: YORK
text: But match to match I have encountered him

speaker: YORK
text: And made a prey for carrion kites and crows

speaker: YORK
text: Even of the bonny beast he loved so well.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Of one or both of us the time is come.

speaker: YORK
text: Hold, Warwick! Seek thee out some other chase,

speaker: YORK
text: For I myself must hunt this deer to death.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Then, nobly, York! ’Tis for a crown thou fight’st.—

speaker: WARWICK
text: As I intend, Clifford, to thrive today,

speaker: WARWICK
text: It grieves my soul to leave thee unassailed.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: What seest thou in me, York? Why dost thou pause?

speaker: YORK
text: With thy brave bearing should I be in love,

speaker: YORK
text: But that thou art so fast mine enemy.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Nor should thy prowess want praise and esteem,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: But that ’tis shown ignobly and in treason.

speaker: YORK
text: So let it help me now against thy sword

speaker: YORK
text: As I in justice and true right express it!

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: My soul and body on the action both!

speaker: YORK
text: A dreadful lay! Address thee instantly.

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: La fin courrone les oeuvres.

speaker: YORK
text: Thus war hath given thee peace, for thou art still.

speaker: YORK
text: Peace with his soul, heaven, if it be thy will!

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: Shame and confusion! All is on the rout.

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: Fear frames disorder, and disorder wounds

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: Where it should guard. O war, thou son of hell,

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: Whom angry heavens do make their minister,

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: Throw in the frozen bosoms of our part

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: Hot coals of vengeance! Let no soldier fly.

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: He that is truly dedicate to war

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: Hath no self-love; nor he that loves himself

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: Hath not essentially, but by circumstance,

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text:
The name of valor.
                 
                He
                 
                sees
                 
                his
                 
                father
                ,
                 
                lying
                 
                dead
                .
               O, let the vile world end

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: And the premised flames of the last day

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: Knit Earth and heaven together!

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: Now let the general trumpet blow his blast,

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: Particularities and petty sounds

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: To cease! Wast thou ordained, dear father,

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: To lose thy youth in peace, and to achieve

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: The silver livery of advisèd age,

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: And, in thy reverence and thy chair-days, thus

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: To die in ruffian battle? Even at this sight

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: My heart is turned to stone, and while ’tis mine,

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: It shall be stony. York not our old men spares;

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: No more will I their babes. Tears virginal

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: Shall be to me even as the dew to fire;

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: And beauty, that the tyrant oft reclaims,

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: Shall to my flaming wrath be oil and flax.

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: Henceforth I will not have to do with pity.

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: Meet I an infant of the house of York,

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: Into as many gobbets will I cut it

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: As wild Medea young Absyrtis did.

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: In cruelty will I seek out my fame.

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: Come, thou new ruin of old Clifford’s house;

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: As did Aeneas old Anchises bear,

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: So bear I thee upon my manly shoulders.

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: But then Aeneas bare a living load,

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: Nothing so heavy as these woes of mine.

speaker: RICHARD
text: So lie thou there.

speaker: RICHARD
text: For underneath an alehouse’ paltry sign,

speaker: RICHARD
text: The Castle in Saint Albans, Somerset

speaker: RICHARD
text: Hath made the wizard famous in his death.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Sword, hold thy temper! Heart, be wrathful still!

speaker: RICHARD
text: Priests pray for enemies, but princes kill.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Away, my lord! You are slow. For shame, away!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Can we outrun the heavens? Good Margaret, stay!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: What are you made of? You’ll nor fight nor fly.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Now is it manhood, wisdom, and defense

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: To give the enemy way, and to secure us

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: By what we can, which can no more but fly.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: If you be ta’en, we then should see the bottom

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Of all our fortunes; but if we haply scape,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: As well we may—if not through your neglect—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: We shall to London get, where you are loved

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And where this breach now in our fortunes made

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: May readily be stopped.

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: But that my heart’s on future mischief set,

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: I would speak blasphemy ere bid you fly;

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: But fly you must. Uncurable discomfit

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: Reigns in the hearts of all our present parts.

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: Away, for your relief! And we will live

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: To see their day and them our fortune give.

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              CLIFFORD
text: Away, my lord, away!
